channel.title: NYT > Business > Energy & Environment
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Is It Time to Tax the Oil and Gas Industry’s Windfall?
item.description: The war in Iran has meant big profits for fossil fuel companies, prompting some lawmakers to call for a tax. But that would be easier said than done.
item.dc:creator: Claire Brown
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gas prices in Washington on Friday.

item.title: More Than 150 Wind Projects Stall as Pentagon Delays Reviews
item.description: The delays, which companies say have worsened significantly in recent weeks, are the latest step in the Trump administration’s efforts to block wind power.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A construction barge being loaded with wind turbine components for construction of a wind farm in Portsmouth, Va., earlier this year.

item.title: Trump Administration Orders Rapid End to Hunting Regulations on Federal Lands
item.description: Internal Interior Department documents show parks are imposing sweeping changes like expanding areas where firearms can be used and allowing game to be cleaned in restrooms.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman
item.category: Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve (La)
item.media:description: Curecanti National Recreation Area in Colorado, where a prohibition on discharging firearms around trails is one of dozens of hunting regulations that will be taken off the books.

item.title: U.S. Warns China Over Iranian Oil as Sanctions Fight Intensifies
item.description: China told its independent refineries to disregard U.S. sanctions over their purchases of Iranian crude.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The warning came from Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, who is expected to participate in high-stakes meetings this month between the United States and China.

item.title: Trump Administration Sues Minnesota to Block Climate Lawsuit
item.description: The Justice Department is seeking to block a lawsuit filed against major players in the fossil fuel industry over their role in climate change.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zraick
item.media:description: Minnesota’s attorney general, Keith Ellison, filed Minnesota’s lawsuit against oil interests six years ago and was named as a defendant in the Trump administration’s lawsuit filed on Monday.

item.title: FEMA Disaster Aid is Flowing Slowly In Trump’s Second Term
item.description: During President Trump’s second term, the disaster declarations that unlock money are taking longer than in the past. Blue states wait the longest and they hear ‘no’ more often.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance and Ashley Cai
item.media:description: A damaged home in Enid, Okla., on April 24, after a tornado ripped through the night before.

item.title: Why U.S. Oil Companies Are Not Plugging the World’s Energy Gap
item.description: American producers are under pressure from investors to keep spending in check, and they are wary of drilling more wells because they are not sure oil prices will stay high.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: J. Emilio Flores for The New York Times
item.media:description: An oil field near Bakersfield, Calif. Western oil companies are reaping the rewards of higher energy prices, but are cautious about digging new wells.

item.title: In Permissive Amsterdam, Ads for Fossil Fuels or Meat Are Now Verboden
item.description: The Dutch city has outlawed advertising that promotes lifestyles linked to high carbon emissions, which is a driver of climate change. It’s a first for a world capital.
item.dc:creator: Cara Buckley

item.title: FEMA Is Reversing Job Cuts Made Under Kristi Noem
item.description: The agency said staff members who had been let go or placed on administrative leave were now needed to prepare for hurricanes and the World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance
item.media:description: Former Homeland Security Kristi Noem during a visit to FEMA headquarters in Washington in January.

item.title: The World Met to Talk Climate Change. The U.S. Wasn’t Invited.
item.description: Dozens of countries met this week to discuss how to end the world’s dependence on fossil fuels, a goal that the Trump administration doesn’t share.
item.dc:creator: David Gelles
item.media:description: Delegates gathered for a meeting on transitioning away from fossil fuels in Santa Marta, Colombia, on Wednesday.

item.title: Smog in Phoenix and Salt Lake City? The E.P.A. Is Blaming Asia.
item.description: The Trump administration says the cities shouldn’t be penalized for unhealthy air because pollution can blow in from abroad. Some experts say that’s preposterous.
item.dc:creator: Hiroko Tabuchi
item.category: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: Phoenix has long struggled with smog collecting in its bowl-shaped topography. The city failed to meet a federal air pollution standard in 2024.

item.title: Forest Service Research Labs Are Closing
item.description: A research lab in Washington State tracks ecological changes in a warming climate and provides scientific guidance for forest managers. It is one of 57 such facilities being shuttered.
item.dc:creator: Eric Niiler and John Stember
item.media:credit: John Stember for The New York Times

item.title: How to Build a Better Kind of Nuclear Power? This Side Hustle Might Help.
item.description: Zap Energy says its ultimate goal is safe, clean energy from fusion. To help get there, it’s starting to build fission reactors.
item.dc:creator: Raymond Zhong
item.media:credit: Grant Hindsley for The New York Times
item.media:description: Benj Conway, Zap’s president and co-founder.

item.title: Loss of Emirates Further Weakens OPEC’s Influence
item.description: The exit of the United Arab Emirates is the most significant in a series of departures from the oil cartel in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Oil storage in Big Spring, Texas. OPEC is less powerful than it once was, in no small part because of the rise of U.S. oil production.

item.title: Global Deforestation Slows, W.R.I. Report Finds. But Wildfires Are Taking a Toll.
item.description: In 2025, the world razed less forest than any other year in the last decade. The bad news: global warming is making wildfires more frequent and intense.
item.dc:creator: Sachi Kitajima Mulkey and Harry Stevens

item.title: The E.P.A.’s Lost Science
item.description: The agency’s prestigious research office is being dismantled by the Trump administration, a plan to dam the Bering Strait and more climate news.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman
item.media:credit: Cornell Watson for The New York Times
item.media:description: Bryan Hubbell, the former head of the air, climate and energy program at the Environmental Protection Agency’s research office. “The state of science is struggling,” he said.

item.title: The World Needs Natural Gas Now, but the U.S. Is Exporting All It Can
item.description: Even the largest global supplier of liquefied natural gas can’t make up for the shortfall since the war in Iran cut off an important source.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The United States is building more export terminals like this one in Cameron, La., but they will take months or years to complete.

item.title: ICE Warehouse Plan Faces Delay Over Lack of Environmental Reviews
item.description: Officials have argued in court filings that the projects are exempt from federally required assessments, but are scrambling after a judge disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:description: A warehouse in Maryland that ICE planned to turn into a migrant detention center, before a court ruling halted the project.

item.title: 60 Countries to Meet on Phasing Out Fossil Fuels but Are Excluding the U.S.
item.description: The Trump administration was not invited to the gathering in Santa Marta, Colombia. A White House spokeswoman called the green transition “destructive.”
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman
item.category: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Dutch and Colombian environment officials at last year’s U.N. climate summit in Belém, Brazil, where this week’s First Conference on Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels in Santa Marta was announced.

item.title: Paddling the Threatened Boundary Waters
item.description: A proposed copper mine in northern Minnesota has become a battleground for politicians and environmentalists — and a pressing reason to explore the waterways.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Pearson
item.media:credit: Aaron Lavinsky/Star Tribune, via Getty Images
